title: Limit dependence on operator precedence
description: Relying too much on operator precedence can make code harder to read and maintain, increasing the risk of subtle bugs. Use parentheses to clarify complex expressions and ensure intended evaluation order. This improves code clarity and reduces maintenance effort.
recommendation: Add parentheses to clarify the intended order of operations in complex expressions.

noncompliant:
int result = a + b * c - d / e; // Noncompliant: unclear precedence
boolean flag = a && b || c && d; // Noncompliant: unclear logic grouping
compliant:
int result = a + (b * c) - (d / e); // Compliant: explicit grouping
boolean flag = (a && b) || (c && d); // Compliant: explicit grouping

checks.message: Add parentheses to clarify the intended order of operations in complex expressions.
